Spend of the good things...), Ibn Jarir narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib regarding the saying of the Exalted: **يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنفِقُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ** (O you who have faith! Spend of the good things you have earned): "He said: 'From gold and silver, and from what We have brought forth for you from the earth.' He said: 'Meaning from grain, dates, and everything upon which there is zakat.'"

In the same source, Ibn Abi Shaybah, Abd ibn Humayd, al-Tirmidhi—who authenticated it—Ibn Majah, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mardawayh, al-Hakim—who authenticated it—and al-Bayhaqi in his *Sunan* narrated from al-Bara ibn Azib regarding the saying: **وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ** (and do not aim at what is bad to spend thereof): "He said: 'It was revealed concerning us, the assembly of the Ansar. We were owners of palm trees; a man would bring from his trees according to their abundance or scarcity. A man would bring a cluster (القنو) or two and hang them in the mosque. The People of the Bench (أهل الصفة) had no food, so when one of them was hungry, he would come to the cluster and strike it with his staff, and unripe and ripe dates would fall for him to eat. Some people who were not desirous of good would bring a cluster containing shriveled and rotten dates (الشيص والحشف), or a broken cluster, and hang it. So Allah revealed: **يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنفِقُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ وَمِمَّا أَخْرَجْنَا لَكُم مِّنَ الْأَرْضِ وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ وَلَسْتُم بِآخِذِيهِ إِلَّا أَن تُغْمِضُوا فِيهِ** (O you who have faith! Spend of the good things you have earned [and of what We bring forth for you from the earth, and do not aim at what is bad to spend thereof, such as you would not take yourselves unless you were to overlook it]). He said: 'If one of you were gifted the like of what he gave, he would not take it except with overlooking (إغماض) and shame.' He said: 'After that, each of us would bring the best of what he had.'"

In *al-Kafi*, from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) regarding the saying of Allah: **يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنفِقُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ وَمِمَّا أَخْرَجْنَا لَكُم مِّنَ الْأَرْضِ وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ** (O you who have faith! Spend of the good things you have earned and of what We bring forth for you from the earth, and do not aim at what is bad to spend thereof): "He said: 'When the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) ordered the palm trees to be taxed (زكاة), some people would bring types of dates that were the worst of dates to pay their zakat—dates called *al-ju'rur* and *al-ma'afarah*, which have little flesh and large pits. Some would bring them instead of good dates. So the Messenger of Allah said: "Do not estimate these two types of palm trees, and do not bring anything from them." Concerning this was revealed: **وَلَا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ مِنْهُ تُنفِقُونَ وَلَسْتُم بِآخِذِيهِ إِلَّا أَن تُغْمِضُوا فِيهِ** (and do not aim at what is bad to spend thereof, such as you would not take yourselves unless you were to overlook it). Overlooking (الإغماض) is that you would take these two [types of] dates.'" In another narration from Abu Abdillah (عليه السلام) regarding the saying of the Exalted: **أَنفِقُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ** (Spend of the good things you have earned), he said: "The people had earned ill-gotten gains in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), so when they embraced Islam, they wanted to remove them from their wealth to give them in charity. But Allah, Blessed and Exalted, refused except that they spend from the best of what they earned."

**I say:** There are many narrations with this meaning from the paths of both groups.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **الشَّيْطَانُ يَعِدُكُمُ الْفَقْرَ** (Satan frightens you of poverty...), he said: "He said: 'Satan says: "Do not spend, for you will become poor," while Allah promises you forgiveness from Him and grace—meaning He forgives you if you spend for Allah, and grace (فضلاً) that He will replace for you.'"

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, al-Tirmidhi—who graded it fair (حسنه)—al-Nasa'i, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Hibban, and al-Bayhaqi in *al-Shu'ab* narrated from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: **ان للشيطان لمة بابن آدم وللملك لمة : فأما لمة الشيطان فإِيعاد بالشر وتكذيب بالحق . وأما لمة الملك فإِيعاد بالخير وتصديق بالحق ، فمن وجد ذلك فليعلم أنه من الله فليحمد الله ، ومن وجد الاخرى فليتعوذ بالله من الشيطان** — 'Indeed, Satan has a touch (لمة) upon the son of Adam, and the angel has a touch. As for the touch of Satan, it is a promise of evil and a denial of the truth. As for the touch of the angel, it is a promise of good and a confirmation of the truth. So whoever finds that, let him know it is from Allah and let him praise Allah; and whoever finds the other, let him seek refuge in Allah from Satan.' Then he recited: **الشَّيْطَانُ يَعِدُكُمُ الْفَقْرَ وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِالْفَحْشَاءِ** (Satan frightens you of poverty and prompts you to indecency...)."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Ayyashi, from Abu Ja'far (عليهما السلام) regarding the saying of the Exalted: **وَمَن يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا** (and he who is given wisdom, is certainly given an abundant good), he said: "It is knowledge (المعرفة)."

In the same source, from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): "Indeed, wisdom (الحكمة) is knowledge and understanding (التفقه) in the religion."

In *al-Kafi*, from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) regarding the verse, he said: "Obedience to Allah and knowledge of the Imam."

**I say:** There are other narrations with this meaning, and they are of the nature of enumerating instances (المصداق).

In *al-Kafi*, from several of our companions from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from some of our companions in a raised (مرفوع) report, he said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Allah has not apportioned anything to the servants better than the intellect (العقل). The sleep of the person of intellect is better than the night-vigil of the ignorant, and the staying of the person of intellect is better than the journeying of the ignorant. Allah did not send any prophet or messenger until he had perfected his intellect and his intellect was superior to all the intellects of his community. What the Prophet conceals in his soul is better than the striving of the strivers. A servant does not fulfill the obligations of Allah until he understands from Him, and all the worshippers do not reach in the excellence of their worship what the person of intellect reaches. The people of intellect are the possessors of understanding (أُولوا الألباب). Allah, Blessed and Exalted, said: **وَمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ** (and no one takes admonition except those who possess intellect).'"

From Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام), who said: "Wisdom (الحكمة) is the light of knowledge, the scale of godwariness (التقوى), and the fruit of truthfulness. If I were to say that Allah has not blessed His servant with a blessing greater, higher, more magnificent, and more splendid than wisdom, I would be correct. Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: **يُؤْتِي الْحِكْمَةَ مَن يَشَاءُ وَمَن يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا وَمَا يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ** (He gives wisdom to whomever He wishes, and he who is given wisdom, is certainly given an abundant good. But no one takes admonition except those who possess intellect)."

**I say:** Regarding the saying of the Exalted: **وَمَا أَنفَقْتُم** (And whatever you may spend...) concerning charity, vows (النذر), and wrongdoing (الظلم), there are many narrations that we will present in their proper places, if Allah wills.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, through several paths from Ibn Abbas, Ibn Jubayr, Asma bint Abi Bakr, and others: "The Messenger of Allah used to forbid charity to those other than the people of Islam, and the Muslims disliked spending on their relatives from among the disbelievers. So Allah revealed: **لَّيْسَ عَلَيْكَ هُدَاهُمْ** (It is not up to you to guide them...) and thus permitted that."

**I say:** It has already been mentioned that His saying "their guidance" (هداهم) is only suitable to mean the guidance of the Muslims present among them, rather than the disbelievers. Thus, the verse is unrelated to what is in the narrations regarding the occasion of revelation. Furthermore, the specification of the recipient in His saying: **لِلْفُقَرَاءِ الَّذِينَ أُحْصِرُوا** (for the poor who are confined...) does not fit it very well. As for the issue of spending on a non-Muslim when it is in the way of Allah and seeking Allah's pleasure, the absolute nature of the verses is sufficient for it.

In *al-Kafi*, from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) regarding the saying of Allah, Mighty and Majestic: **وَإِن تُخْفُوهَا وَتُؤْتُوهَا الْفُقَرَاءَ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ** (but if you conceal them and give them to the poor, that is better for you), he said: "This is other than zakat; indeed, zakat is public, not secret."

In the same source, from him (عليه السلام): "Everything that Allah has made obligatory upon you, its public performance is better than its concealment; and whatever is voluntary (تطوعاً), its concealment is better than its public performance."

**I say:** There are other traditions with the meaning of these two hadiths, and that which clarifies their meaning has already preceded.

In *al-Majma'*, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **لِلْفُقَرَاءِ الَّذِينَ أُحْصِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ** ( [The charities are] for the poor who are confined in the way of Allah...), he said: "Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام) said: 'The verse was revealed concerning the People of the Bench (أصحاب الصفة).'" He said: "And similarly, al-Kalbi narrated it from Ibn Abbas. They were about four hundred men who had no dwellings in Medina, nor clans to whom they could seek shelter. So they placed themselves in the mosque and said: 'We will go out in every expedition (سرية) that the Messenger of Allah sends.' So Allah urged the people toward them; a man, when he ate and had surplus, would bring it to them when evening came."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Ayyashi, from Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام): "Indeed, Allah hates the importunate (الملحف)."

In *al-Majma'*, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ** (Those who spend their wealth by night and day...), he said: "The cause of revelation (سبب النزول) from Ibn Abbas: 'This verse was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib (عليه السلام). He had four dirhams with him; he gave one in charity by night, one by day, one secretly, and one openly. So it was revealed: **الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً** (Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and openly).'" al-Tabarsi said: "And this is what is narrated from Abu Ja'far and Abu Abdillah (عليهما السلام)."

**I say:** This meaning was narrated by al-Ayyashi in his *Tafsir*, al-Mufid in *al-Ikhtisas*, and al-Saduq in *al-Uyun*.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Abd al-Razzaq, Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Tabarani, and Ibn Asakir narrated through the path of Abd al-Wahhab ibn Mujahid from his father from Ibn Abbas regarding the saying of the Exalted: **الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً** (Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and openly), he said: "It was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib (عليه السلام). He had four dirhams, so he spent a dirham by night, a dirham by day, a dirham secretly, and a dirham openly."

In *Tafsir al-Burhan*, from Ibn Shahrashub in *al-Manaqib*, from Ibn Abbas, al-Suddi, Mujahid, al-Kalbi, Abu Salih, al-Wahidi, al-Tusi, al-Tha'labi, al-Tabarsi, al-Mawardi, al-Qushayri, al-Thumali, al-Naqqash, al-Fattal, Abdullah ibn al-Husayn, and Ali ibn Harb al-Ta'i in their commentaries: "That Ibn Abi Talib had silver dirhams, so he gave one in charity by night, one by day, one secretly, and one openly. So there was revealed: **الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُم بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً** (Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and openly). Thus, He called each dirham 'wealth' (مالاً) and gave him the glad tidings of acceptance."

In some commentaries: "The verse was revealed concerning Abu Bakr; he gave forty thousand dinars in charity: ten by night, ten by day, ten secretly, and ten openly."

**I say:** Al-Alusi mentioned in his commentary under this hadith: "Imam al-Suyuti followed this by stating that the report of his spending forty thousand dinars was only narrated by Ibn Asakir in his *History* from Aisha, and it contains no mention of the revelation of the verse. It seems that whoever claimed that understood it from what Ibn al-Mundhir narrated from Ibn Ishaq, who said: 'When Abu Bakr passed away and Umar was appointed successor, he addressed the people, praised Allah and extolled Him as He deserves, then said: "O people, indeed some greed is poverty, and some despair is wealth. You gather what you do not eat, and you hope for what you do not reach. Know that some stinginess is a branch of hypocrisy. So spend good for your souls. Where are the companions of this verse?" and he recited the noble verse.' And you know that there is no indication in this of its revelation in his favor." (End of quote).

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, through several paths from Abu Umamah, Abu al-Darda, Ibn Abbas, and others: "The verse was revealed concerning the owners of horses (أصحاب الخيل)."

**I say:** What is meant by them are the frontier-guards (المرابطون) who spend on horses night and day. However, the wording of the verse—namely His saying "secretly and openly"—does not apply to it, as there is no meaning for this generalization and alternation in spending on horses at all.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur* as well, al-Musayyib narrated: "The verse **الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ** (Those who spend...) in its entirety was revealed concerning Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and Uthman ibn Affan regarding their spending on the Army of Hardship (جيش العسرة)."

**I say:** The problem with this, in terms of non-applicability, is similar to the problem in the previous one.
